tweets.text: .@SecBlinken: Standing for people’s freedom and dignity honors America’s most sacred values. https://t.co/I84I4fE8pz

.@SecBlinken: We will bring to bear all the tools of our diplomacy to defend human rights and hold accountable perpetrators of abuse. The reports we’re releasing are just one way we can do that. #HumanRightsReport https://t.co/tevZB5pGqS

.@SecBlinken: The lives of people in Syria depend on getting urgent help. We have to do everything in our power to create ways for that aid to get to them – to open pathways, not to close them. https://t.co/IF2qJA1zhH https://t.co/QuUAS5rcAm

.@StateDeptSpox on #TDOV: The United States stands with the international community to celebrate the courage and resilience of transgender and gender non-conforming persons around the world and to acknowledge their efforts to achieve equality and justice in the face of adversity. https://t.co/PZQLkUOK9s
.@StateDeptSpox: Equitable access to vaccines is critical to reduce the tragic loss of life, end the pandemic, recover the U.S. and global economy, and keep Americans safe. https://t.co/Djy4I5skOm

.@StateDeptSpox: The United States will continue to be a leader in the humanitarian response and to advocate for unhindered humanitarian access to Syrians regardless of where they live. https://t.co/2UNbPIEDOy

.@StateDeptSpox: The United States stands united with our allies and partners in speaking out for the human rights and freedoms of Hong Kongers, and we call on the PRC to uphold its international obligations and commitments under the Sino-British Joint Declaration. https://t.co/JWGa2vrcpH

.@StateDeptSpox: We call for the full and unconditional release of all political prisoners & the cessation of violence by the authorities against the Belarusian people. The United States will continue to support the Belarusian people in their aspirations for a democratic future. https://t.co/dDe17DKU7a
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/KZOciWg1i1

Today at approximately 1:45 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/zFg1iz3WQB, https://t.co/6RJHUn7v4A, and https://t.co/d5yzYVtlo3. https://t.co/zCt2tPiFDJ

On this final day of #WomensHistoryMonth, @NMADMuseum honors the diplomatic legacy of philanthropist, businesswoman, and collector Marjorie Merriweather Post for her contributions to diplomacy. Check out the latest DipNote for more: https://t.co/oSUfegYbN1

.@SecBlinken on the humanitarian situation in Syria: We have to find a way to do something to take action to help people. That is our responsibility, and shame on us if we don’t meet it. https://t.co/z9jrG8W5Yx
.@SecBlinken: We have a responsibility to ensure Syrians have access to lifesaving assistance, no matter where they live. https://t.co/n4ZwH9njQG
Today, we celebrate the visibility of trans and non-binary people in society and advocate for their human rights. Trans rights = human rights. #TDOV https://t.co/63J6eyhhFL

On #TransDayofVisibility, we recognize the inspiring courage of trans and non-binary people and the countless contributions they've made to communities around the world. We will continue to promote equality, inclusion, and respect for the human rights of all trans people. https://t.co/GbnTmNRB6j
.@SecBlinken: The @UN is the anchor of the multilateral system. And that multilateral system is vitally important to the United States. https://t.co/odp88cKKb5

.@PressSec: Burmese Security Forces have killed hundreds of people in Burma since they perpetrated a coup on Feb. 1st. We will continue to impose costs on the military regime for the bloodshed and horrific violence against peaceful protesters and the suppression of human rights. https://t.co/whfOpJ4N6w
.@SecBlinken: The @UN, as the leading multilateral institution in the world, has a major role to play in meeting the global challenges of our time. https://t.co/0U9JDRasMI

.@StateDRL Acting Assistant Secretary Lisa Peterson: Our aim is always to identify human rights challenges and use our voice and position on the world stage to draw attention to abuses of human rights, no matter where or when they occur. https://t.co/f9covSeva8

.@SecBlinken on Burma: We continue to call on the military regime to release all who’ve been unjustly detained; stop its attacks on civil society members, journalists & labor unions; halt the killings by its security forces; and return to power the democratically elected gov’t. https://t.co/L7jtNKmVfM

.@SecBlinken on the release of #HumanRightsReport: @POTUS has committed to putting human rights back at the heart of our foreign policy. We will bring to bear all the tools of our diplomacy to defend human rights and hold accountable perpetrators of abuse. https://t.co/AUTMw0uWJT

Today, @SecBlinken rolled out the 45th annual Human Rights Report. #HRR2020 consists of 198 country reports that show progress since last year - or lack of progress by some governments - with respect to human rights & fundamental freedoms. https://t.co/TRsldrKVJL
Live now! @SecBlinken and @StateDRL Acting Assistant Secretary Lisa Peterson deliver remarks on the 2020 Country Reports on Human Rights Practices. https://t.co/huJlagTlJw

Today at approx. 2:00 p.m. EDT, @SecBlinken and @StateDRL Acting Assistant Secretary Lisa Peterson will deliver remarks on the 2020 Country Reports on Human Rights Practices. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/khhoISVjEq. https://t.co/QSEJ0PoTKK
.@SecBlinken: And at our best, American leadership at the UN helps make all these things happen.

.@SecBlinken: At its best, I know the United Nations to be a place where countries coordinate a global response to crises like COVID-19 and climate change, stand together to defend universal human rights, and uphold the rules-based order that maintains stability and security. https://t.co/2jJvIenEgQ

As @SecBlinken has previously stated, we stand with the Asian-American community and against hate. In the wake of recent acts of violence in Atlanta, @USEmbassyBKK Charge d'Affaires Michael Heath shares his story and how we can stamp out injustice. https://t.co/4MD1LVF1Re
.@POTUS reaffirmed the U.S. commitment to values-based global leadership and re-engagement with international institutions, in particular the @UN. https://t.co/3qheit2x8X
FACT: Through the @UN, the United States collaborates with over 190 members. Learn more: https://t.co/JFnyxzxX6j. https://t.co/W5zcGFSzzv
.@POTUS Biden: We will be a strong and trusted partner for peace, progress, and security. https://t.co/o2iJjfOWQi https://t.co/fJ9xOmlMzN

Live now! @SecBlinken meets with @UN Secretary-General @antonioguterres. The Secretary will then meet with President of the UN General Assembly @volkan_bozkir & conclude his virtual trip with a press briefing with @USAmbUN Thomas-Greenfield. https://t.co/yyE7YoMKTF

.@VP Kamala Harris: The U.S. is strengthening our engagement with the @UN. We are also rejoining the Human Rights Council, because we know the status of democracy depends on our collective commitment to those values articulated in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. https://t.co/DEQ8lJiYfb
If you missed @SecBlinken’s remarks this morning at the virtual town hall with staff from @USUN, watch them on YouTube at https://t.co/FBKuRz1Gve.
.@SecBlinken: Not a single one of the really big problems that we face…not a single one can be met fully by any one country acting alone. https://t.co/o9gRBxwl23
At approximately 10:00 a.m. EDT,  @SecBlinken will chair a @UN Security Council briefing and consultations on the humanitarian situation in Syria. Watch live at https://t.co/ZAYUURFe9K.
.@SecBlinken: Our administration is committed to leading with diplomacy to advance the interests of the United States and to strengthen the rules-based international order. https://t.co/GsVHJ2gSyY
At approx. 9:15 a.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will deliver remarks ahead of a virtual town hall with staff from @USUN. Watch live on https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz. https://t.co/LkGSoTs69e
https://t.co/Iub6nesEQg
Today, @SecBlinken will travel virtually to the @UN. Follow along on his trip here on Twitter and https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz. Find a schedule of events below. https://t.co/mPbaLuIElf
.@POTUS: When we strengthen our alliances, we amplify our power as well as our ability to disrupt threats before they can reach our shores. https://t.co/VSfk7Qrtyb

.@SecBlinken: Americans look at the threats we face – like climate change, the COVID-19 pandemic, economic inequality, an increasingly assertive China – and they know that the United States is much better off tackling them with partners, rather than trying to do it alone. https://t.co/PzylYjbCyc
America’s alliances are our greatest asset, and leading with diplomacy means standing shoulder-to-shoulder with our allies and key partners once again. https://t.co/HncpV7TygO

.@SecBlinken: Great trip to Brussels. As strategic competitors continue to challenge us, @NATO binds North America and Europe in the collective defense of our people and our values. I was glad to reaffirm U.S. support for the Alliance. https://t.co/UfiqRdLWbJ
#HappyHoli! We wish all of those who are celebrating a happy and colorful time filled with love and peace. May this time bring you all goodness and prosperity. https://t.co/6LwzuHjcWr

.@SecBlinken: We must modernize our alliances...That means teaming up to develop cutting-edge innovations, ensuring that our sensitive supply chains are resilient, setting the norms & standards that will govern emerging technologies, and imposing costs on those who break rules. https://t.co/eiB1KFQPJJ

.@SecBlinken: We must weave together broader coalitions of allies and partners. The more that countries with complementary strengths and capacities can unite to achieve shared goals, the better. https://t.co/zpl6j2BJDb https://t.co/sI8fMPCOnS
Chag sameach! Today, we wish all who are celebrating a happy and healthy #Passover! May this time be one of renewed commitment to peace and freedom. https://t.co/VmHqayzgyZ

Have you read the latest on our official blog, DipNote? Recent entries spotlight 60 years with Global Ties, new security partnerships with Sudan, and improving airport security worldwide. https://t.co/87Rfebg8BB

On March 31 at 10:30 a.m. EDT, join #IWOC2021 awardees Canan Gullu, Muskan Khatun, and Zahra Mohamed Ahmad for a discussion with @ElliottSchoolGW. All are invited to hear about these courageous women's experiences. RSVP here: https://t.co/MN8mT2tF1T. #WomenOfCourage https://t.co/D5CvybF54v

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeputySpox condemned the PRC's campaign against international companies, congratulated Bangladesh on its 50th anniversary of independence, and recognized @GlobalTiesUS for 60 years of exchanges. Read more: https://t.co/CKCOWcVdLU

.@POTUS: Elevating the status of women and girls globally is the right thing to do — it is a matter of justice, fairness, and decency, and it will lead to a better, more secure, and more prosperous world for us all. https://t.co/rPTyOARtCy https://t.co/cC9h8aySQv

For 60 years, @GlobalTiesUS and its Network of more than 80 non-profit organizations have worked with us to facilitate exchange programs and foster international people-to-people ties. Learn more in our latest DipNote ⬇️. #ExchangeMatters #60YearsOfImpact https://t.co/m3TFyc2Qvw

This month and every month we continue to promote gender equality and celebrate women of all backgrounds. The status of democracy depends on the empowerment of women. #WomensHistoryMonth https://t.co/gwRnUd6ueg
.@SecBlinken: When we tear down barriers that are holding women back, we make economies stronger for everyone. https://t.co/93tOEH73iY
From stopping COVID-19 to renewing democracy, we need countries to cooperate, now more than ever. https://t.co/QbB0EgKQvT
.@POTUS: America’s alliances are among our greatest assets. And leading with diplomacy means standing shoulder to shoulder with our allies and key partners once more. https://t.co/FQZvuqi28c
.@POTUS: The simple truth is, America cannot afford to be absent any longer on the world stage. America is back. Diplomacy is back. Alliances are back. https://t.co/ubQf1kX4RN

.@SecBlinken: We must modernize our alliances...That means teaming up to develop cutting-edge innovations, ensuring that our sensitive supply chains are resilient, setting the norms & standards that will govern emerging technologies, and imposing costs on those who break rules. https://t.co/p5flkW9xyf

.@SecBlinken: We need alliances – now as much as ever. The challenge we face is to adapt and renew those alliances so they can meet today’s threats, and continue to deliver for our people now, as they have in the past. https://t.co/fLpHMMnu4w

.@SecBlinken: Americans look at the threats we face – like climate change, the COVID-19 pandemic, economic inequality, an increasingly assertive China – and they know that the United States is much better off tackling them with partners, rather than trying to do it alone. https://t.co/uUovcdo04T
.@SecBlinken: “We must recommit to our alliances – and to the shared values that sustain them.” Read more: https://t.co/zpl6j2BJDb. https://t.co/uPifG1xOEg
Today, as Belarusians celebrate Freedom Day, we share their hopes for Belarus’ democratic future and stand in solidarity with them. https://t.co/ynIIj6fDMh

.@SecBlinken: Even as we work with Russia to advance our interests and advance alliance interests, we’ll also work to hold Russia to account for its reckless and adversarial actions. https://t.co/sEQjh7yNlB
.@SecBlinken: @NATO has been the cornerstone of transatlantic security for more than 70 years. The United States is committed to this Alliance now and in the future. https://t.co/09iH3p0PSN
.@SecBlinken: We’re determined to revitalize our alliances, to revitalize our partnerships, starting with NATO. #WeAreNATO https://t.co/MUvViPxIfR

Join @NMADmuseum today at 5:00 p.m. EDT for a prerecorded Diplomacy After Hours program on the legacy of U.S. Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg. To hear more about her contributions to diplomacy, visit https://t.co/lVsMidjTM4 #WomensHistoryMonth

Today is World Tuberculosis (TB) Day. TB is the deadliest infectious disease in low and middle-income countries, and COVID-19 has exacerbated its global impact. The U.S. government is committed to supporting our partners in the fight to end the disease: https://t.co/C2QYTXD0y1. https://t.co/sdVydpK98m
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers a foreign policy speech on alliances and partnerships. https://t.co/cgDcyIIWuW

Today at 10:00 a.m. EDT (3:00 p.m. CET), @SecBlinken will deliver a foreign policy speech on alliances and partnerships. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/GwJpdSyZTs. https://t.co/DV3BmPuDsO
Live now! @SecBlinken hosts a press conference from Brussels, Belgium. https://t.co/FhyOrYwH8O

Today at 8:00 a.m. EDT (1:00 p.m. CET), @SecBlinken will host a press conference from Brussels, Belgium. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/kNrvXo33yb. https://t.co/asxJPmwuOx

.@SecBlinken: Whether it is tackling some of the new challenges like climate, in the cyber realm, the rise of autocratic states and the challenges they pose – we have a profound interest in doing it together, doing it collectively, relying on collective security. https://t.co/wMExAcVNYN

.@SecBlinken on @NATO: It is a remarkable institution bringing together now 30 countries. We come together around shared values of democracy, freedom, openness, and a rules-based international order to make sure that countries can move forward together peacefully. https://t.co/yg6IJpPoyj

When it comes to COVID-19 vaccine distribution, the U.S.-Belgium partnership is a key component. Learn more about our work together as scientists from the United States and Europe lead the world’s fight against the pandemic: https://t.co/aA9oz13f7D.

The United States and Sudan are working to develop security partnerships, as Sudanese leaders build a democratic, citizen-centered government. Two of our diplomats assigned to @USAfricaCommand and @USNavyEurope discuss these efforts in the latest DipNote: https://t.co/vTNArZgDna

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeputySpox highlighted @SecBlinken’s trip to Brussels, including the @NATO Foreign Ministerial, and commented on the @UN_HRC resolution to promote human rights in Sri Lanka. Read the transcript for more.
https://t.co/e1iOehsZiM

.@SecBlinken: The United States wants to rebuild our partnerships, first and foremost, with our NATO allies.  We want to revitalize the alliance to make sure it’s as strong and effective against the threats of today as it has been in the past. https://t.co/xxcn1L2vKe

Belgium is a close @NATO Ally and a partner in security and defense, making valuable contributions to the Global Coalition to Defeat ISIS and NATO’s Resolute Support Mission in Afghanistan. Learn more about U.S.-Belgium relations: https://t.co/GM63XVpQpb. https://t.co/8zPD5o8XBg
Together, the U.S. and the EU have pledged more than $40 billion to the global pandemic response. That far surpasses all other donors. https://t.co/pH9TuHzXnE

The EU is one of our closest partners. U.S.-EU cooperation on the basis of our shared values is crucial to uphold the rules-based international order and address shared challenges including Iran, Russia, and China. https://t.co/T8FmBsQVPO

.@NATO is the essential forum for Transatlantic security. The U.S. commitment to our NATO Allies and Article 5 is ironclad. Read more on our partnership: https://t.co/GM63XVpQpb. https://t.co/QYqzFmo20O
.@SecBlinken: The United States is determined to strengthen the Transatlantic Alliance and renew global relationships. https://t.co/vtknIbxRNk
Live now! @SecBlinken joins @NATO Secretary General @jensstoltenberg for a moderated conversation on “Strengthening the Transatlantic Bond”.   https://t.co/HvowUesVbK

Today at 5:00 a.m. EDT (10:00 a.m. CET), @SecBlinken will join @NATO Secretary General @jensstoltenberg for a moderated conversation on “Strengthening the Transatlantic Bond." Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/XTh8yIs2Te. https://t.co/KWpieLXerI
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks to the press with @NATO Secretary General @jensstoltenberg.  https://t.co/pedonP5Oue

Today at 3:50 a.m. EDT (8:50 a.m. CET), @SecBlinken will deliver remarks to the press with @NATO Secretary General @jensstoltenberg. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7, and https://t.co/fTC9gzgjf6. https://t.co/IlALynfC1X
.@POTUS: Hate can have no safe harbor in America. It should have no safe harbor anywhere in the world. We must join together to make it stop. https://t.co/YhtyjaGCoi

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeputySpox discussed the appointment of Ricardo Zuñiga as Special Envoy for the Northern Triangle, a renewed commitment to a ceasefire in Yemen, and an attack on a hospital in Syria. Read the transcript: https://t.co/GSwLuRKcTK
In our latest DipNote blog, @StateDeptCT shares how they are working to improve aviation security worldwide. Have a read: https://t.co/XWHzgpetsz.
Not a single global challenge can be met by any one nation acting alone. The United States stands shoulder-to-shoulder with our @NATO allies. #WeAreNATO https://t.co/CQUSliAc04
.@POTUS: The United States is fully committed to our NATO Alliance, and I welcome Europe’s growing investment in the military capabilities that enable our shared defense. https://t.co/CwbqkBZpP1

.@POTUS: The United States will work closely with our European Union partners and the capitals across the continent — from Rome to Riga — to meet the range of shared challenges we face. We continue to support the goal of a Europe whole and free and at peace. https://t.co/KbEn3DocUk
The U.S. is committed to building a more water-secure world and celebrates those working to ensure safe water for all. #WorldWaterDay https://t.co/uHt7JB3raw

On #WorldWaterDay, learn how the United States is working with partners around the world to strengthen water security through improved water management and by restoring and protecting healthy ecosystems to build resilience to climate change. https://t.co/nkwHgiZ8wB https://t.co/mfd094NKMe

.@StateEUR Acting Assistant Secretary Reeker previews @SecBlinken’s travel to Brussels. The Secretary will attend the @NATO Foreign Ministerial and meet with EU leaders and Belgian officials to discuss strengthening ties to confront global challenges. https://t.co/od8UGRkSjf
.@SecBlinken: We’re going to make sure that we have a Department that actually looks like the country it represents. https://t.co/tryBZ0BqtX https://t.co/QikIyC4BH9

Have you read the latest on our official blog, DipNote? Recent entries take a deep dive into geomagnetism, explore how the Department is empowering women in peace and security, and commemorate the 10th anniversary of the Great East Japan Earthquake. https://t.co/87RfebxJt9
.@SecBlinken: We imposed Russia sanctions on Aleksey Navalny's poisoning and detention. And we imposed sanctions in response to the military coup in Burma.

.@SecBlinken: We released the Khashoggi report and announced the Khashoggi Ban to stop people who engaged in serious, extraterritorial counter-dissident activities on behalf of a foreign government from entering our country.

.@SecBlinken on democracy and human rights: We joined the UN Human Rights Council as an observer. We put into effect the presidential proclamation that U.S. diplomacy protect the human rights of LGBTQI people worldwide. https://t.co/MKTJDc5EBS

The United States is committed to the international organizations that shape our world. Our partnerships promote collective action to protect the planet, foster inclusive governance, and advance peace, prosperity, and a respect for human rights. https://t.co/KU8p8wrvQC

Today as we mark International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination, we affirm that all are created equal and free. We will continue to #fightracism and defend human rights for all. https://t.co/HXyl02eTBQ

.@SecBlinken: Diversity and inclusion make us stronger, smarter, more creative, and more innovative. And our diversity gives us a significant competitive advantage on the world stage. https://t.co/6hRHShSwed https://t.co/WokArf5b8A

.@SecBlinken: A hallmark of our leadership, of our engagement in the world is our alliances and our partnerships that have been built on a totally voluntary basis. And it is something that President Biden is committed to reinvigorating. https://t.co/2gnGV6YQeD

.@VP Kamala Harris: The U.S. is strengthening our engagement with the @UN. We are also rejoining the Human Rights Council, because we know the status of democracy depends on our collective commitment to those values articulated in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. #CSW65 https://t.co/9RGj8TQHHp
.@POTUS: America’s alliances are among our greatest assets. And leading with diplomacy means standing shoulder to shoulder with our allies and key partners once more. https://t.co/bRniPh5CKV
.@SecBlinken: Our administration is committed to leading with diplomacy to advance the interests of the United States and to strengthen the rules-based international order. https://t.co/xM1p7Vqr0M

The U.S. Department of State extends #Nowruz greetings to all those celebrating this joyous occasion and the arrival of Spring. It is a time to reflect and look forward to brighter days to come. https://t.co/TU5rhBthXo https://t.co/Lyy8rkvxPt
America’s alliances are our greatest asset, and leading with diplomacy means standing shoulder-to-shoulder with our allies and key partners once again. https://t.co/tWNoCxexCr

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeputySpox commented on Under Secretary Hale’s announcement of over $80 million in humanitarian assistance for the Sahel region of Africa & took questions on Secretary Blinken’s visit to East Asia and Alaska. https://t.co/ZoCsXP9PNU

Have you read the latest on our official blog, DipNote? Recent entries spotlight the 10th anniversary of the Great East Japan Earthquake, a deep dive into geomagnetism, and how the Department is empowering women in peace and security. https://t.co/87RfebxJt9

.@POTUS: The simple truth is, America cannot afford to be absent any longer on the world stage. And under the Biden-Harris Administration, America is back. Diplomacy is back. Alliances are back. https://t.co/R4dIR7b6t9 https://t.co/1321sV4q9a
.@SecBlinken on COVID-19: We re-engaged the @WHO and contributed an initial $2 billion to COVAX, the global vaccination initiative. https://t.co/DvMq9ZjY2P https://t.co/oBbOKGGZJz

.@SecBlinken: The U.S.-Republic of Korea Alliance is, as we have long said, the linchpin of peace, security, and prosperity in Northeast Asia, the Indo-Pacific, and around the world. https://t.co/14F24AtzNn

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeputySpox previewed @SecBlinken’s meetings in Alaska, welcomed the announcement of a new UN envoy to Afghanistan, and commented on assistance for Ethiopia’s Tigray region. Read the transcript for more. https://t.co/SA02oyvocr

.@SecBlinken: We’re deeply invested in re-engaging with and reinvigorating our alliances, and that includes not only our relationship to our allies but the relationship of our allies to one another. That’s an important part of this alliance system. https://t.co/xG3w0w14js

.@SecBlinken: "The United States stands united with our allies and partners in speaking out for the rights and freedoms of people in Hong Kong, and we will respond when the People's Republic of China fails to meet its obligations." Read more: https://t.co/8jch6Z8OCV. https://t.co/Ln2VJ57oj9

Have you read the latest on our official blog, DipNote? Recent entries spotlight the 10th anniversary of the Great East Japan Earthquake, a deep dive into geomagnetism, and how the Department is empowering women in peace and security. https://t.co/87Rfebg8BB

Today is #GlobalRecyclingDay. Learn about @StateGDI's sustainable practices that reduce the Department’s environmental footprint, increase operational security and efficiency, and facilitate healthier spaces for employees and populations across the globe. https://t.co/4aLHS71uuf

.@SecBlinken: We’re deeply invested in re-engaging with and reinvigorating our alliances, and that includes not only our relationship to our allies but the relationship of our allies to one another. That’s an important part of this alliance system. https://t.co/8VyzrfrFuD

.@SecBlinken: "The United States stands united with our allies and partners in speaking out for the rights and freedoms of people in Hong Kong, and we will respond when the People's Republic of China fails to meet its obligations." Read more: https://t.co/8jch6Z8OCV. https://t.co/VgqkpfoKn0

The pandemic also had significant economic impacts on the world economy. We need to lead a sustainable recovery – and even better, a green recovery. Because that’s the best way to meet the climate goals we share. https://t.co/267IWSCko2 https://t.co/iPaqCP77oL

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeputySpox discussed Under Secretary Hale’s virtual trilateral meeting with Afghanistan Foreign Minister Mohammed Haneef Atmar and Tajikistan Foreign Minister Sirojiddin Muhriddin. Read more: https://t.co/R3fUo1X5wJ.

The U.S.-ROK’s strong economic ties benefit the U.S. middle class and the U.S. economy. ROK foreign direct investment in the U.S. has more than tripled from $19.7 billion in 2011 to $61.1 billion in 2019. Learn more: https://t.co/gJNJNJwBmd. https://t.co/8Fz1NJ66Tj

The U.S.-Republic of Korea Alliance is the linchpin of peace, security, and prosperity for Northeast Asia, a free and open Indo-Pacific region, and across the world. Our alliance are essential to addressing today's challenges. Learn more here: https://t.co/yaBNCjXUg4. https://t.co/MVq2JPMfR0

Happy #StPatricksDay! Today, we also celebrate Irish-American Heritage Month and the strong bilateral relationship and deep cultural ties between the United States and Ireland. Read more about our partnership: https://t.co/hMoWMl9h4M. https://t.co/6dEfytqFGb

.@VP Kamala Harris: The U.S. is strengthening our engagement with the @UN. We are also rejoining the Human Rights Council, because we know the status of democracy depends on our collective commitment to those values articulated in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. #CSW65 https://t.co/ejAUQdkGAC

The Biden Administration has recommitted the United States to a foreign policy centered on democracy, human rights, and equality.
We imposed Russia sanctions on Alexei Navalny’s poisoning and detention. And we imposed sanctions in response to the military coup in Burma.

We released the Khashoggi report and announced the Khashoggi Ban to stop people who engaged in serious, extraterritorial counter-dissident activities on behalf of a foreign government from entering our country.
